name: env_continuity_bloat_walk
description: Catastrophic regression detector for env-fact continuity bloat during repeated movement. Walks east 7 times from a fixed env-rich founding premise. Each movement turn asserts continuity_block_chars < 2500 — a generous threshold calibrated for first-run use with no baseline data. Tighten the threshold after a successful run reveals actual steady-state values. Failure here means dedup has regressed severely (pre-dedup behavior produced ~1700+ chars after 8 walks; catastrophic regression would push well past 2500). The < 2500 ceiling is intentionally not ambitious — it detects complete dedup breakdown, not mild drift.
world_prompt: A quiet town on a warm afternoon. Main Street has cracked pavement, faded facades with peeling paint, a red-and-white awning over a shop door, and a rusted mailbox with its flag at half-mast. Weeds push through the concrete seams.

turns.action: I am standing on Main Street in a quiet town, in front of a row of weathered buildings with cracked pavement and a red-and-white awning. A rusted mailbox stands crooked nearby.
